architecture.summary: CLAIRify combines large language models with automatic iterative prompting and program verification to translate natural language chemistry experiment instructions into domain-specific language (DSL) programs. The LLM generates candidate plans; a verifier checks syntactic validity and constraint satisfaction; invalid plans trigger re-prompting with error feedback. Valid DSL programs are then executed by a PDDLStream-based constrained task and motion planner that solves for collision-free and spill-free robot trajectories in a physics-aware simulation before real-world deployment.

biblio.title: Large language models for chemistry robotics

claims.statement: CLAIRify successfully generates syntactically valid domain-specific language programs for chemistry experiments through automatic iterative prompting with program verification, incorporating environmental constraints

The approach demonstrates planning and execution of chemistry experiments on a real robot, including pouring skills for various materials and two fundamental chemical experiments: solubility and recrystallization
PDDLStream solvers are used to prevent spillages of liquids and collisions when executing generated plans in chemistry labs
The framework adds a natural language interface to autonomous chemistry experiment systems to lower the barrier for non-expert users and increase utility
